channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk Ends Hunger Strike After Government Assurances
item.description: The article discusses the end of a hunger strike by environmental activist Sonam Wangchuk in Delhi, which he concluded after receiving written assurances from the government regarding reforms to the N...

item.title: UK Economy Surpasses Growth Rates of Previous Decades
item.description: The UK's economy is experiencing its fastest growth in a decade, driven by a surge in consumer spending and business investment. Recent data from the Office for National Statistics (ONS) indicates tha...

item.title: Security forces conduct successful operation in Mastung, neutralizing four terrorists
item.description: In a significant security operation in Balochistan's Mastung district, Pakistani security forces have killed four terrorists and recovered weapons during a coordinated effort. The operation, confirmed...

item.title: AIADMK Leader Criticizes Tamil Nadu Government Over Unannounced Holiday for Movie Release
item.description: Senior AIADMK leader R B Udayakumar accused the Tamil Nadu government of implementing an unannounced holiday across the state to celebrate the release of Chief Minister M.K. Stalin's movie 'Jana Nayag...

item.title: Iten Court Lifts Fencing Orders in Kaptagat Forest Case, Allows Community Involvement
item.description: The Environment and Land Court in Iten has taken a significant step by admitting Community Forest Associations (CFAs) as interested parties in the ongoing legal battle over Kaptagat Forest fencing. Th...

item.title: Nigeria's Largest Meth Lab Seized in Federal Court Visit
item.description: A Nigerian federal court conducted an inspection of a clandestine methamphetamine laboratory in Ogun State, which is linked to the country's largest drug seizure operation. The visit followed the arre...

item.title: Hudson Young faces long recovery after Achilles injury, Raiders deal with additional setbacks
item.description: Hudson Young, a key player for the Canberra Raiders, has suffered a severe Achilles injury, ruling him out of the NRL season and jeopardizing his World Cup prospects. The injury occurred during a matc...

item.title: Daily Horoscope for July 24, 2026: Insights for Each Zodiac Sign
item.description: The article provides daily horoscope predictions for each zodiac sign on July 24, 2026. ARIES is advised to seek common ground in disputes, while TAURUS may uncover opportunities through Mercury-Venus...

item.title: Pastor Convicted of Stealing KSh 44m from Churches
item.description: A pastor in Louisiana, Dale Sanders, has been found guilty of diverting over KSh 44 million from two Baptist churches he led between 2020 and 2024. Prosecutors revealed he used the stolen funds for pe...

item.title: Durban Lifeguards Demonstrate Water Safety Skills Ahead of World Drowning Prevention Day
item.description: Durban lifeguards will demonstrate their essential skills at the Rachel Finlayson Swimming Pool on Friday to commemorate World Drowning Prevention Day on Saturday. The event, organized by the eThekwin...

item.title: Southport Murderer Transferred to High-Security Psychiatric Care After Mass Attack
item.description: Axel Rudakubana, the perpetrator of a deadly attack at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in Southport that killed three children and injured nine others, has been transferred from HMP Belmarsh to Broa...

item.title: Manila's Decline in QS Best Student Cities Rankings
item.description: Manila has slipped out of the top 100 in the latest QS Best Student Cities Rankings, dropping to 102nd place in the 2027 edition released by Quacquarelli Symonds (QS). This marks a significant decline...
